title: Investigate Rust source-extraction + lock per-test RED disposition

source_code_change.rationale: Per dispatch-contract.json M1 scope, source-code change is CONDITIONAL on finding an additive resolution for Rust .unwrap chain source-extraction. M1 empirical analysis shows: (a) tldr taint already extracts Rust source variables correctly via find_parent_assignment_var on the `let x = ...` parent assignment — no .unwrap() descent is needed; (b) the actual root cause for Rust 4 RED tests is a CLI dispatch bypass at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 (analyze_file routes .rs files to analyze_rust_file line scanner, bypassing scan_vulnerabilities). This bypass is out of M1's files_modified clause. No 0-30 LOC fix in taint.rs would have any behavioral effect on Rust 4 RED test outcomes.

routing_summary.breakdown: M2 = S(2) + K(16) + B(5) + T-python_xss(1) = 24. M3 = T-js_xss + T-ts_xss = 2. M4 = F-js_deser + F-ts_deser = 2. M5-carry-forward = X(5) = 5. Sum = 24 + 2 + 2 + 5 = 33.

rust_disposition.rationale_short: tldr taint extracts source/sink/flow correctly for all 4 Rust RED tests, but vuln.rs:368-370 routes .rs files away from scan_vulnerabilities. Fix requires CLI dispatch change in crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs, out of M1/M2/M3/M4 scope.

pre_flight_grep_summary.vuln_type_projection_hits: 4 hits — all in string-literal FP fixtures (path_traversal_string_literal_fp.{js,ts}); no actionable findings.
pre_flight_grep_summary.direct_sink_type_variant_hits: 11 active assertions in rr_framework_integ_test.rs (FileWrite filter) + 1 benign variant-enum hit in security_tests.rs:327. Of the 11 assertions, THREE exercise `.send` shapes that M3 reclassifies (FileWrite -> HtmlOutput): lines 168 (fastify reply.send), 248 (nestjs res.send), 301 (nestjs response.send builder lowercase form). The remaining 8 cover non-send shapes (.redirect, .header, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, NextResponse, etc.) which M3 KEEPS as FileWrite per spec.
pre_flight_grep_summary.premortem_completeness: Premortem E1 identified 2 of 3 send-tests. M1 surfaced the third: nestjs_response_builder_send_via_compute_taint at line 301. M3 dispatch contract should be amended to update THREE assertions, not two.

follow_up_for_orchestrator: M3 dispatch contract scope mentions 2 test assertion updates; M1 evidence shows 3. Update the M3 contract's files_modified clause and stop_thresholds before launching M3 worker.

Carry-forward count is 5 (1 Ruby + 4 Rust), at the cap. The dispatch contract's success_summary projects '165/166 passing'; with M1's revised carry-forward count, post-M5 will be 161/166 passing. M6 CHANGELOG entry should reflect the corrected count.

If a post-M6 follow-up milestone wants to close the Rust 4 carry-forward, it needs to design how analyze_rust_file's UnsafeCode/Panic findings interact with scan_vulnerabilities's taint findings — out of vuln-source-parity-v1 scope.
